checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=67174469.5 (2013-09-30) vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=70862721.6 (2014-09-30), +5.5% YoY
SalesRevenueServicesNet=126720138.02 (Q3 2013) vs SalesRevenueServicesNet=136790227.91 (Q3 2014), +8.0% YoY

AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=147707962.35 (2011-12-31) vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=135447065.23 (2012-12-31), declining while SalesRevenueServicesNet=514277022.2 (2011) grew to SalesRevenueServicesNet=531876083.41 (2012)

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=123349525.74 (2010-12-31) growing to Goodwill=141200883.72 (2014-09-30), approx +14.5% over the period
SalesRevenueNet=455088925.36 (2010) growing to annualized run-rate near SalesRevenueServicesNet=136790227.91 (Q3 2014) implying revenue growth roughly in line with or exceeding goodwill growth
OtherAssetsNoncurrent=28456897.86 (2010-12-31) fell to OtherAssetsNoncurrent=15145066.7 (2011-12-31), then fluctuated in the 15-32M range through 2014, no sustained runaway growth

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=100635555.86 (2011) vs NetIncomeLoss=39915527.54 (2011)
NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=96466306.31 (2012) vs NetIncomeLoss=47729467.15 (2012)
NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=55746696.25 (2013) vs NetIncomeLoss=23639663.07 (2013)

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No InventoryNet or comparable inventory concept reported; company appears to be a services business (SalesRevenueServicesNet, CostOfServices tags)

Gross margin computed from SalesRevenueServicesNet=514277022.2 (2011) and CostOfServices=187990137.0 (2011) ~63.4%, declining to SalesRevenueServicesNet=540259959.61 (2013) and CostOfServices=212475629.54 (2013) ~60.7%

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-119795.59 (Q1 2014) following NetIncomeLoss=3922398.03 (Q4 2013) and NetIncomeLoss=4554047.5 (Q3 2013), showing marked quarter-to-quarter volatility including a quarterly loss
OperatingIncomeLoss=36301.69 (Q1 2014) vs OperatingIncomeLoss=17766049.0 (Q2 2013) - large swings quarter to quarter

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AccountsPayableCurrent=6614168.63 (2010-12-31) fell to AccountsPayableCurrent=2219848.58 (2011-12-31) and remained near AccountsPayableCurrent=1858646.73 (2013-12-31) despite revenue growing from SalesRevenueNet=455088925.36 (2010) to SalesRevenueServicesNet=540259959.61 (2013)

AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=66223365.12 (2010-12-31) declined to AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=54594117.47 (2012-12-31) and AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=60752699.84 (2013-12-31), roughly flat-to-down while revenue grew ~19% cumulatively over the period

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-K filed 2007-03-16, followed by NT 10-Q filings on 2007-05-10, 2007-08-09, and 2007-11-09 - a cluster of late-filing notifications within a single year
10-K/A filed 2012-03-12 shortly after the original 10-K filed 2012-03-09, and 10-Q/A filings on 2006-02-24 and 2006-02-28
Repeated CORRESP and UPLOAD (SEC comment letter correspondence) filings recurring in 2006, 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, and 2013 suggest ongoing SEC review of disclosures across multiple years

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data spans fiscal years 2009 through Q3 2014 across Assets, Liabilities, Revenue, NetIncomeLoss, and cash flow statement line items, providing roughly 5-6 years of trend data
Revenue reporting tag changes across filings (Revenues, SalesRevenueNet, SalesRevenueServicesNet) create minor discontinuities in the time series that require reconciliation

top_signals: Accounts payable and accrued liabilities balances stagnated or declined (AccountsPayableCurrent=6614168.63 (2010-12-31) down to 1858646.73 (2013-12-31); AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=66223365.12 (2010-12-31) down to 60752699.84 (2013-12-31)) despite revenue growth from ~455M (2010) to ~540M (2013), raising a mild question about reserve/accrual adequacy though plausibly explained by a services-industry cost structure with low vendor payables.

Profitability deteriorated markedly faster than revenue: NetIncomeLoss fell from 39915527.54 (2011) to 47729467.15 (2012) to 23639663.07 (2013) and turned negative in Q1 2014 (-119795.59), while SG&A grew faster than revenue (SellingGeneralAndAdministrativeExpense up from 266739401.6 (2011) to 302532871.79 (2013) vs revenue growth of only ~5%), consistent with genuine margin compression rather than clear manipulation given cash flow patterns remained supportive.

Historical filing irregularities (NT 10-K/NT 10-Q cluster in 2007, 10-K/A and 10-Q/A amendments, and recurring SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD correspondence across multiple years) indicate a pattern of past disclosure scrutiny, though these are largely dated relative to the 2014 cutoff and not corroborated by irregularities in the 2011-2014 filings themselves.

No corroborating red flags found in receivables-to-revenue growth, cash-flow-to-earnings divergence, or earnings smoothness -- these traditional manipulation indicators are largely clean, which tempers overall concern despite the liability and margin signals noted above.
